page: Microsoft Azure FX64-16ms_v2 is a memory-intensive server from the FXmsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. Hardware specifications include an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, consisting of 8 physical cores and 2 threads per core. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the processor features a 260 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with a massive 1344.0 GB of RAM, yielding 84.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is absent, and the instance does not include GPU hardware, though it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates exceptional single-core capabilities. Geekbench single-core scores, single-threaded gzip compression, and text processing throughput all rank in the top 10% tier. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is also strong, placing in the top 25% tier. Other metrics, such as multi-core stress-ng performance, memory read bandwidths, and medium-to-large LLM inference speeds, align with average tier expectations. These results highlight a system optimized for high-frequency single-threaded execution and rapid memory write operations.

Given the extremely high memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance offers a specialized hardware profile tailored for memory-bound workloads rather than massive parallel compute scaling. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and data-heavy processing tasks where massive RAM capacity and high single-core performance are prioritized over local storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure FX64-16ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server from the FXmsv2 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and an exceptionally large 1344.0 GB RAM capacity. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this Hyper-V virtualized instance delivers 84.0 GB of memory per core. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it supports premium external storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores reaching the top 10% tier, alongside strong memory write bandwidth. This profile makes the server highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, such as large-scale in-memory databases, heavy text processing, and enterprise applications requiring massive memory density relative to compute cores.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX64-16ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 1344.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core and text processing performance.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring massive RAM density, high-frequency Intel Xeon processing, and top-tier single-core performance for enterprise workloads.

Massive 1344.0 GB RAM capacity providing 84.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier single-core performance and single-threaded gzip compression speeds
